prerequisites: A Teamtailor account with access to API keys (Settings > Integrations > API keys at https://app.teamtailor.com/app/settings/integrations/api-keys).
A Teamtailor API key with sufficient scope/permissions (Public, Internal, or Admin; read, write, or read/write) for the data you intend to read.
The API version date to send as the X-Api-Version header, which is required on every request (you can use today's date to always get the latest API version).
Knowledge of which Teamtailor stack hosts your data so requests target the correct base URL (api.teamtailor.com for the EU stack, api.na.teamtailor.com for the NA stack).

authMethods.name: API key (Authorization: Token token)
authMethods.summary: Teamtailor authenticates by passing your secret API key in the HTTP Authorization header using the format 'Authorization: Token token=<api_key>'. Provide this key via the connector's required 'api' config field. Generate and manage keys under Settings > Integrations > API keys in the Teamtailor app.

setupSteps.title: Create a Teamtailor API key
setupSteps.body: In Teamtailor, go to Settings > Integrations > API keys (https://app.teamtailor.com/app/settings/integrations/api-keys) and create an API key. Choose a scope and permission level (Public, Internal, or Admin with read, write, or read/write) that covers the data you need. Copy the generated key; it is your secret credential.

setupSteps.title: Provide the API key
setupSteps.body: Set the required 'api' config field to your Teamtailor API key. The connector authenticates by sending it in the Authorization header as 'Token token=<api_key>'. Keep this value secret.

setupSteps.title: Set the API version
setupSteps.body: Set the required 'x_api_version' config field. Teamtailor requires the X-Api-Version header on every request. Use a dated version (you can use today's date to always get the latest version of the API), per the API changelog of current and old versions.

setupSteps.title: Confirm the correct base URL for your stack
setupSteps.body: Teamtailor's Public API base URL depends on the stack where your data is stored: use api.teamtailor.com for the EU stack and api.na.teamtailor.com for the NA stack. Ensure requests target the host matching your account's region.

sources.title: Teamtailor app - Manage your API keys (Settings > Integrations > API keys)
